---
title: "fglrx, HD 3450, Samsung 1080p and massive amounts of underscan"
date: 2008-12-20
forum: Hardware
---

### Post by voteforpedro on 2008-12-20
Right, I'm getting pretty annoyed now. The open source radeon/ati driver (default) gives me a perfect 1920x1080@60Hz screen. I can't use this driver though because it's for a HTPC and the driver can't render the DVB video fast enough. fglrx, for all its faults, gives great live video playback but MASSIVE BLACK BORDERS around the desktop.

I feel like a fool because I had it working after days of messing about three weeks ago, then decided I wanted Mythbuntu instead. I then couldn't remember how I fixed it so went back to Ubuntu 8.10 i386 and still can't figure it out.

As said, the problem is that there is a massive black border around my 40" screen, probably about 2" to the left AND right and slightly less on the top and bottom. This is more annoying than unusable, but I would like to use every pixel of my new 40" 1080p television. I mean, who wouldn't?

The television is set to screen mode "Just Scan" which is the same as 1:1 pixel mapping or dot-for-dot or whatever the other TVs call it. If I click the "i" button the remote, it confirms the signal is 1920x1080@60Hz.

The problem, therefore, is that the fglrx signal is adding the underscan. I can confirm this like so:

```
$ aticonfig --query-dispattrib=tmds2i
Query monitors tmds2i ,Cap:0xff
Supported adjustment type for tmds2i : brightness, contrast, saturation, hue, positionX, positionY, sizeX, sizeY 

 brightness attribute information of monitor tmds2i :
 default:0, value:0, min:-100, max:100, step:1

 contrast attribute information of monitor tmds2i :
 default:100, value:100, min:0, max:200, step:1

 saturation attribute information of monitor tmds2i :
 default:100, value:100, min:0, max:200, step:1

 hue attribute information of monitor tmds2i :
 default:0, value:0, min:-30, max:30, step:1

 positionX attribute information of monitor tmds2i :
 default:76, **value:76**, min:0, max:1920, step:1

 positionY attribute information of monitor tmds2i :
 default:43, **value:43**, min:0, max:1080, step:1

 sizeX attribute information of monitor tmds2i :
 default:1767, **value:1767**, min:1129, max:1920, step:1

 sizeY attribute information of monitor tmds2i :
 default:994, **value:994**, min:635, max:1080, step:1
```

I have highlighted the key bits of info there: position X and Y are not zero, but 76 and 43. The resolution is also less than 1920x1080 despite being set in gnome and also forced via my xorg.conf. These values clearly explain the underscan - but how can it be solved?

```
$ aticonfig --set-pcs-val=MCIL,DigitalHDTVDefaultOverscan,0
```

This command does NOTHING. Yes, it changes a value in my /etc/ati/amdpcsdb file but has no effect whatsoever on the resolution.

```
$ aticonfig --set-dispattrib=tmds2i,positionX:0
$ aticonfig --set-dispattrib=tmds2i,positionY:0
$ aticonfig --set-dispattrib=tmds2i,sizeX:1920
$ aticonfig --set-dispattrib=tmds2i,sizeY:1080
```

This works. I see before my vary eyes the screen positioning and resizing itself correctly. But as soon as I logout and back in, I get no screen. In fact, X crashes as the computer does not show up in my router's attached devices list nor can I ssh to it. I then must enter recovery mode and do:

```
$ aticonfig --initial --force
```

or

```
$ sudo cp /etc/ati/amdpscdb.default /etc/ati/amdpcsdb
```

And then I'm back to square one.

Any suggestions? I am THIS close to giving up and going to buy an NVIDIA card and will avoid ever buying an ATI device again due to their shocking support for Linux, or lack thereof.

---

### Post by voteforpedro on 2008-12-20
/etc/X11/xorg.conf:

```
Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "aticonfig Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]-0" 0 0
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "Module"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]-0"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "aticonfig-Device[0]-0"
	Driver      "fglrx"
	Option	    "UseFastTLS" "1"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "aticonfig-Screen[0]-0"
	Device     "aticonfig-Device[0]-0"
	Monitor    "aticonfig-Monitor[0]-0"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
	EndSubSection
EndSection
```

---


---

### Post by yct on 2009-02-01
Hi, don't know what Samsung you've got, I have LE32A559 and the latest ATI driver, and I've got it working this way:

(my video card is ATI Technologies Inc RV670PRO [Radeon HD 3850])

1. sudo rm /etc/ati/amdpcsdb; sudo aticonfig -f --initial

2. reboot (actually, best is to press the RESET button)

3. launch ATI control panel, configure whatever you need

4. sudo aticonfig --set-pcs-val=MCIL,DigitalHDTVDefaultUnderscan,1

5. sudo cp /etc/ati/amdpcsdb /etc/ati/amdpcsdb.1

6. create an executable file /etc/init.d/samsung.sh with contents:
```

#!/bin/sh

### BEGIN INIT INFO
# Provides:          samsung
# Required-Start:    $atieventsd
# Required-Stop:     $atieventsd
# Default-Start:     2 3 4 5
# Default-Stop:      0 1 6
# Short-Description: Start daemon at boot time
# Description:       Enable service provided by daemon.
### END INIT INFO

/bin/cp -f /etc/ati/amdpcsdb.1 /etc/ati/amdpcsdb

```

7. sudo update-rc.d /etc/init.d/samsung.sh defaults

8. create an executable file /etc/init.d/samsung1.sh with contents:
```

#!/bin/sh
aticonfig --set-dispattrib=tmds2i,positionX:0
aticonfig --set-dispattrib=tmds2i,positionY:0
aticonfig --set-dispattrib=tmds2i,sizeX:1920
aticonfig --set-dispattrib=tmds2i,sizeY:1080

```

9. make sure the samsung1.sh file is called when your X starts, ie. in Gnome, add it to the System->Preferences->Sessions.

10. reboot!

The workaround is basically to undo whatever changes happen to the amdpcsdb file after the option DigitalHDTVDefaultUnderscan was added.
I know you said that option didn't do anything, but it actually influences how the --set-dispattrib options work.

Also check your Samsung's "Just Scan" option - make sure to press the RESET related to it.

---

### Post by mrv on 2009-04-06
Catalyst 9.3 seems to finally fix the issue, allowing the amount of underscan to be controlled via catalyst control center. No more need for init scripts, resetting to defaults or running aticonfig manually on login.

---
